commands.review_summary:
The branch expands implicit `message_tool_only` source-reply routing beyond webchat, copies reply-payload metadata through `/usage` footer appends, and adds an opt-in Telegram live QA scenario.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +92. Total +141 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main gates the internal source-reply sink to webchat, while the linked report and PR test path show Telegram `message_tool_only` replies fall off the decorated final-payload path. I did not see successful live Telegram proof yet.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor/maintainer handling for live Telegram proof, the protected `maintainer` label, and the mirror-text finding; missing real behavior proof cannot be supplied by a repair-only lane.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve /usage footer for tool-only replies
target.body:
Fixes #87392.

## Summary
- route implicit `message_tool_only` sends with non-webchat current source context through the internal source-reply sink
- keep explicit `channel` / `target` / `to` / `channelId` / `targets` sends on the normal outbound path
- preserve reply payload WeakMap metadata when appending `/usage` footer text so source-reply delivery and transcript mirror metadata survive the payload replacement
- add an opt-in private Telegram live QA scenario, `telegram-tool-only-usage-footer`, for real Telegram proof of this bug

## Public surface
- No config surface changes.
- No plugin public contract/API changes.

## Proof
Source-level dry-run behavior covered by tests:
- Telegram `message_tool_only` send with `currentChannelProvider=telegram`, `currentChannelId`, and `currentMessageId` now returns `handledBy: "internal-source"`, so the reply remains on the final source-reply payload path where `/usage` decoration runs.
- Non-webchat provider context without a source address still does not infer the internal sink and continues to require an explicit target.
- Explicit routed sends still stay on the normal outbound path via existing coverage.
- `appendUsageLine` now copies reply payload metadata when replacing the text payload, preserving source-reply suppression delivery and transcript mirror metadata.

Real Telegram proof status:
- Added opt-in scenario `telegram-tool-only-usage-footer`; it sends `/usage tokens`, then a Telegram mentioned agent prompt, and asserts the observed SUT reply contains both the exact QA marker and `Usage:` in one SUT message.
- Mantis native Telegram Desktop proof was attempted twice via Crabbox (`aws` run 26540926434 and `hetzner` run 26541503033), but both failed before exercising the code because the desktop test machine/environment could not be allocated.
- Next proof run should use `Mantis Telegram Live` with scenario `telegram-tool-only-usage-footer`, which exercises real Telegram without requiring native desktop allocation.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.test.ts --reporter=dot` (4 files, 97 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts --reporter=dot` (1 file, 37 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/telegram/telegram-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Note: commit hook was bypassed because this symlinked WSL worktree triggers the known no-TTY `pnpm install` modules-purge prompt. The equivalent focused checks above were run directly.
